drift_catches: §16.36 (no flag, success-confirmation) — Both briefs this batch passed §16.36 statutory-cite cross-check WITHOUT new drift catches. Pick 1 (SSE) — TCGA 1992 Sch 7AC paras 1 + 3 + 3A + 5 + 7 + 7A + 15A + 19 + 20 all verified stable; TCGA 1992 s.171 + s.171A + s.179 verified; FA 2002 s.44 + Sch 8 + F(No.2)A 2017 s.27 + Sch 5 + FA 2011 Sch 10 verified. Pick 2 (transferring-business-out) — TCGA 1992 s.17 + s.122 + s.165 + s.179 + CTA 2010 s.1030A + Part 23 + CA 2006 ss.1003-1010 + FA 2003 s.43 + Sch 4 + Sch 4ZA + VATA 1994 s.81(3) + Sch 4 para 5 + SI 1995/2518 regs 112-116 + CAA 2001 s.61 + s.198 + FA 2012 s.34 + Sch 4 + FA 2013 Sch 14 (historic s.162B) all verified. **CRITICAL — F-11 D-13 phoenix-TAAR cite-correction propagated correctly:** pick 2 carries the corrected ITTOIA 2005 s.396B (NOT ITA 2007 s.396B) cite throughout Statutory anchor + Worked examples + FAQs + do-not-write GREP discipline. Stage 1 seed of pick 2 also carried the correct cite — no recurrence of the F-11 §16.40 drift pattern across the M2-A-B3 → M2-A-B4 transition. This is the FIRST consecutive batch where no statutory-cite drift catch occurred since F-7 (M2-A-B1). Pattern: where Stage 1 seed authors have internalised prior-batch correction discipline, Stage 2 §16.36 cross-check serves as confirmation gate rather than catch gate. Both modes earn the gate's keep at the ~13% brief-quality error rate baseline.

§16.40 (housekeeping, no flag) — Pick 1 (SSE) Stage 1 seed flagged HIGHEST-RISK verification point for RUN session: Sch 7AC para 7A QII subsidiary-eligibility tests for property holdings (Example 2). The QII route's 'interest in land' carve-out may DISQUALIFY property-development subsidiaries from QII protection even where conventional trading test would pass. Stage 2 brief extension explicitly flags this for RUN-session WebFetch verification before writing the QII section + provides fallback framing ('QII operative for institutional-fund-owned HoldCos disposing of NON-PROPERTY trading subsidiaries within broader portfolio') if RUN session confirms the carve-out applies. This is a RUN-session-flagged verification risk, not a Stage 2 drift catch.

discoveries_logged: D-17 SIXTH CONSECUTIVE STAGE-2 BATCH §16.36 CROSS-CHECK CYCLE — F-11 §16.40 D-13 phoenix-TAAR ITTOIA-2005-s.396B cite-correction propagated cleanly from batch M2-A-B3 into batch M2-A-B4 Stage 1 seed (pick 2). This is the FIRST batch in the §16.40 pattern series (F-7, F-8, F-9, F-10, F-11) where no new drift catch arose at Stage 2. Pattern observation: Stage 1 seed authors who have internalised prior-batch corrections produce clean Stage 1 output that requires Stage 2 §16.36 as a CONFIRMATION gate rather than a CATCH gate. Both modes earn the gate's keep at the ~13% brief-quality error rate baseline. The §16.36 statutory-cross-check workflow step is now structurally embedded as a non-optional Stage 2 discipline. Future Stage 1 dispatch prompts could enumerate the known-drift watchpoints (s.464C/D omitted from s.455 mechanic; s.131 routes via s.152 not direct; PM276000 = CIS not salaried-member; PM236500 does not exist; ITTOIA 2005 s.396B not ITA 2007 s.396B) as a worked-example pre-emptive correction list to reduce Stage 2 catch rate further.

anti_templating_check: Two briefs read as a contrasting pair within Bucket A's entity-exit cluster. Pick 1 (SSE) is a DEEPER STATUTORY-MECHANICS REFERENCE LAYER for advisor / FD audience anchored to one specific corporate-tax exit mechanism (Schedule 7AC family of exemptions). Pick 2 (entity-exit decision frame) is a BROAD COMPARISON FRAMEWORK across all 5 exit routes for property founders / FDs / advisors at the strategic-decision layer. Worked examples are deliberately non-overlapping: pick 1 examples (multi-DevCo HoldCo + QII institutional-fund + s.179 degrouping + loss-symmetry + asset-strip-para-5) all live within the SSE-specific mechanics layer; pick 2 examples (£500k-through-5-routes comparison + strike-off hybrid + phoenix TAAR + bona vacantia + VAT CGS clawback) all live at the decision-frame / route-comparison layer. Anonymised personas are distinct across the two picks (Threadwell + Marshfield Capital + Eastfield + Pilcrow + Cranbridge for SSE; Frampton + Dunlevy + Burnham + Hatherfield + Vorlund for entity-exit). FAQ counts differ (12 for SSE; 14 for entity-exit reflecting decision-frame breadth). Do-not-write GREP discipline patterns differ (13 for SSE focused on Sch 7AC mechanics; 16 for entity-exit covering 5-route comparison breadth). No template-feel; each pick holds its distinctive operational angle.
cross_pick_coordination_check: Pick 1 (SSE) sits at the SPECIALIST-DEPTH layer for one exit route; pick 2 (entity-exit decision frame) sits at the DECISION-FRAME layer covering 5 routes including SSE as one option. Pick 2 explicitly forward-links to pick 1 for SSE-route depth + s.179 degrouping deeper mechanics. Pick 1's s.179 degrouping section explicitly references the multi-company group operation lock §21.8 (LIVE post F-3 batch M2-A-B2) which is also load-bearing for pick 2's degrouping discussion. Both picks reference the §28 trading-vs-investment Wave 8 cluster (Pawson investment-line analysis informing SSE trading-company test eligibility + investment-LtdCo CGT-on-share-sale framing in pick 2's Option B). Both picks reference Pawson + HMRC CG53116 consistently. Both picks reference the existing user-facing substantial-shareholding-exemption-property-companies page (pick 1 as PRIMARY anti-cannibal target; pick 2 as one of multiple specialist depth pages). No cross-pick content duplication detected; the relationship is decision-frame → specialist-depth pillar→specialist consistent.

NO new flags raised this batch (clean batch).
